rationale: This example shows the base-catalyzed cleavage of a simple, acyclic beta-keto ester (ethyl 2-methyl-3-oxopentanoate) into a ketone (butan-2-one) and an ester (ethyl acetate). This is a classic textbook illustration of the retro-Claisen reaction, demonstrating the characteristic C-C bond cleavage between the alpha and beta carbonyl carbons.
collision_avoidance_note: The structure is a simple acyclic beta-keto ester, avoiding complex ring systems, aromatic groups, or other reactive functional groups that could trigger unrelated named reaction queries. The transformation is a straightforward cleavage, distinct from condensation, acylation, or alkylation reactions.
